# Joey Lopez
> A pastebin of useful things — prompt templates, teaching materials, security research, workshop kits.

## Code

- [Dev Second Brain](https://jrlopez.dev/p/bootcamp-skills.html#tab-dev): ReAct-driven code migrations, refactoring, debugging.

- [TL Second Brain](https://jrlopez.dev/p/bootcamp-skills.html#tab-tl): ADRs, metaprompts, spike plans, .cursorrules.

- [prompt-kit](https://github.com/josephrobertlopez/prompt-kit): The prompt templates I actually use. Clone and adapt.

- [Aperiodic Guardrails Repo](https://github.com/josephrobertlopez/aperiodic-guardrails): Source code + 91 pattern analysis dataset.

## Methodology

- [Agent-First Website Design](https://jrlopez.dev/p/agent-first-design.html): Your website has two audiences now. One of them doesn't have eyes. Standards, patterns, implementation.

- [Make Skills (Capstone)](https://jrlopez.dev/p/bootcamp-skills.html#tab-make): Build your own skill from any repeated task.

- [Lattice-Driven Development](https://jrlopez.dev/p/lattice-dev.html): Dependency-ordered dev. Build L1 before L2.

- [Diagrams as Prompts](https://jrlopez.dev/p/mermaid-prompts.html): Mermaid diagrams as structured reasoning inputs.

- [Prompts Are Programs](https://jrlopez.dev/p/prompt-cheatsheet.html): Composition cheat sheet. Load/Chain/Compose operators.

## Prompting

- [AI Learning Hub](https://jrlopez.dev/p/ai-learning-hub.html): Guided path: why AI → tools → patterns → pick your track.

- [PO/PM Second Brain](https://jrlopez.dev/p/bootcamp-skills.html#tab-po): User stories, backlogs, roadmaps, exec reports.

- [DL Second Brain](https://jrlopez.dev/p/bootcamp-skills.html#tab-dl): Priorities, risk matrices, status reports, onboarding.

- [Bootcamp Skills](https://jrlopez.dev/p/bootcamp-skills.html): 5 role-specific skills — Swagger-style with copy buttons.

- [Session 2 — Advanced](https://jrlopez.dev/p/bootcamp-session2.html): ReAct, Tree of Thoughts, spec-kit, interview prep.

- [Facilitator Guide](https://jrlopez.dev/p/bootcamp-facilitator.html): Minute-by-minute script for running the workshop.

- [Session 1 — Patterns](https://jrlopez.dev/p/bootcamp-session1.html): Three Approaches, foundational patterns, priority builder.

- [Bootcamp Prereq](https://jrlopez.dev/p/bootcamp-prereq.html): 15-minute self-study before the live session.

- [Prompt Engineering Bootcamp](https://jrlopez.dev/p/prompt-engineering-bootcamp.html): 90-minute hands-on workshop — systematic AI workflows, role-specific tracks, capstone skill build.

- [Prompt Patterns (Advanced)](https://jrlopez.dev/p/prompting-advanced.html): ReAct, Tree of Thoughts, self-consistency, constitutional AI.

- [Prompt Patterns (Foundational)](https://jrlopez.dev/p/prompting.html): Zero-shot, few-shot, chain-of-thought, persona, template.

## Reference

- [About](https://jrlopez.dev/p/about.html): CHSPE → community college → UC Irvine → data engineering → formal proofs via AI agents.

- [Resume](https://jrlopez.dev/p/resume.html): Sr. Data Engineer — 5 years financial services.

- [Quick Reference Cards](https://jrlopez.dev/p/bootcamp-reference.html): 6-card one-pager of all frameworks.

## Security

- [Guardrails Deep Dive](https://jrlopez.dev/p/guardrails-deep-dive.html): Interactive walkthrough of the proof.

- [Guardrails for Engineers](https://jrlopez.dev/p/guardrails-engineers.html): The paper for engineers — no PhD required.

- [Your Regex Guardrails Are Provably Blind](https://github.com/josephrobertlopez/aperiodic-guardrails/blob/main/paper/main.pdf): Formal proof that regex safety filters have a fatal algebraic blind spot.
